---
title: "Codebooks"
---

A codebook is the set of codes used to annotate source text.

## Codes

Codes represent concepts, themes, labels, or categories. Each code has a name and colour. Code colours are used for highlights and chips in the coding interface.

## Folders

Folders group related codes. Folders are for organisation only; annotations are applied to codes, not folders.

## Reordering

The order of codes matters because ACE assigns quick keyboard positions from the visible code order. Put frequently used codes near the top.

## Sharing a codebook

For team projects, agree on the codebook before independent coding begins. If coders use different codebook versions, agreement results can be harder to interpret.
